description:
<description>
        You are on call as an operations engineer.  
        An application outage that occurred on 2024-06-15 generated a small bundle of diagnostic
        material that now lives in the directory

            /home/user/incidents/2024-06-15/

        Inside that directory are exactly three plaintext files:

            • app.log  
            • sys.log  
            • readme.txt  

        1. Package the entire 2024-06-15 incident directory (and nothing else) into a single **gzip-compressed tar archive**.  
        2. Save the resulting archive in **/home/user/archives/**.  
        3. The resulting file must be named exactly

               2024-06-15_logs.tar.gz

        Archive layout requirements that the automated test will enforce:

            • When the archive is listed with
                  tar -tzf /home/user/archives/2024-06-15_logs.tar.gz
              the only paths that may appear are
                  2024-06-15/
                  2024-06-15/app.log
                  2024-06-15/sys.log
                  2024-06-15/readme.txt

            • The leading directory inside the archive must therefore be literally “2024-06-15/”.
            • No absolute paths are allowed in the archive; every member must be relative to the archive root.
            • The archive file itself must exist at the exact path and name given above.

        You do NOT need to print or modify any of the log contents; simply create the correctly-structured
        compressed archive.  One well-chosen terminal command is enough.
    </description>
truth:
<!-- Files that already exist before the task starts -->
    <preexisting>
        <dir path="/home/user/incidents/2024-06-15/">
            <file name="app.log">
                <![CDATA[
2024-06-15 00:00:01 INFO  Starting application bootstrap
2024-06-15 00:00:07 ERROR Failed to connect to database
                ]]>
            </file>
            <file name="sys.log">
                <![CDATA[
Jun 15 00:00:02 kernel: memory allocator initialised
Jun 15 00:00:08 kernel: stack trace follows
                ]]>
            </file>
            <file name="readme.txt">
                <![CDATA[
These logs were captured automatically by the incident response tooling.
They may contain sensitive information – handle with care.
                ]]>
            </file>
        </dir>
        <dir path="/home/user/archives/" />
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Artefacts that must exist after the agent finishes -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/archives/2024-06-15_logs.tar.gz">
            <tarball>
                <member>2024-06-15/</member>
                <member>2024-06-15/app.log</member>
                <member>2024-06-15/sys.log</member>
                <member>2024-06-15/readme.txt</member>
            </tarball>
        </file>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_e28cb093
